prompt_rules: eight-frame motion study for ONE direction only, arranged in a 4x2 grid: TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right
high-resolution 16-bit-plus pixel art, each frame about 256x256, character drawn LARGE filling most of the cell height with a small margin
follow the supplied motion phases exactly
same character identity, facing and scale in every cell

prompt:
Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER SWORD ATTACK sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing UP (PURE BACK VIEW: we see only his BACK - the back of his head with the small low ponytail, the back of the indigo haori, and both shoulders and arms. NO face, NO chest, the torso never twists to a side or front view). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. He holds his short ritual sword with BOTH HANDS together on the hilt, the blade pointing straight AWAY from the viewer (into the screen / toward the top of the cell). CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions and body width of the attached reference (his own walking sheet) EXACTLY - same head-to-body ratio, same total height; body at about 70 percent of cell height. KEEP THE POSE SIMPLE AND CLEAN: this is a back-view strike where fine detail is barely visible, so the only goal is that it clearly reads as a natural TWO-HANDED forward swing with NO awkwardness. 8-frame simple two-handed forward chop, back always to the viewer: frame1 = both hands hold the hilt centered in front of him, blade pointing away; frame2 = small windup, both arms draw back slightly (blade still points away); frame3 = both arms push the sword FORWARD away from the viewer; frame4 = both arms extended forward, blade pointing away along the ground ahead - the key pose; frame5 = begin settling; frame6 = settle; frame7 = return toward the centered two-hand hold; frame8 = centered two-hand hold (loops). BOTH hands stay together on the hilt in EVERY frame and both arms move together symmetrically and stay close to the body - NEVER one hand, NEVER an idle hand left hanging at the side, NEVER arms pointing in opposite directions. Do NOT raise the blade high overhead, do NOT draw an elaborate wind-up, do NOT show the blade edge prominently - keep it a small, clean, natural back-facing two-handed forward strike. NO motion trail, NO swoosh, NO afterimage, NO energy effect, NO glow: draw ONLY the solid body and the solid sword in every frame. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, feet on the same ground line in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
